scope: Mixed high-impact batch focused on top unprojected returners/transfers plus selected freshmen/international prospects with role evidence.
method: Prioritize players who can materially change the top of the smart leaderboard.
Use projected-lineup reporting, roster retention, awards/watch-list context, school roster notes, transfer reports, and recruiting or international profile signals.
Keep returning players anchored to proven college production unless role, award, and usage evidence justify a proven-star lift.
Keep transfers anchored to historical rating unless the new roster gives them a clearly larger role.
For freshmen and internationals, separate talent from role: strong entry talent does not automatically imply top-board projected BBPR without clean minutes and usage evidence.
Use the site calibration layer for newcomer soft caps and proven-player lifts when the batch is reviewed or applied.

formulas.projectedBbprWithHistory: historicalRating/proven production blended with projection score, then calibrated for proven-star or transfer-role context
formulas.projectedBbprNewcomer: projection score compressed through newcomer credibility and soft cap range

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Use Yes only with projected-lineup evidence, returning starter evidence, or role-lock reporting. Use Uncertain for likely rotation players without clean starting evidence.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: 95-100 for national-player or primary-option evidence, 88-94 for high-end starters, 80-87 for likely starters or high-minute newcomers, and 70-79 for rotation/newcomer uncertainty.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Reward returning usage growth, roster departures, and clear depth-chart openings. Penalize crowded frontcourts/backcourts and injury uncertainty.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Reflect likely usage hierarchy, not just talent. Defensive anchors and low-usage centers can have high role scores but lower burden.
teamContextRubric.freshmanInternationalAdjustment: Elite freshmen/internationals can score highly, but only role certainty separates them from the broader newcomer range.

players.researchSummary: Fears is the cleanest unprojected returning-star profile in this batch: elite historical anchor, projected Michigan State lead guard role, preseason Big Ten/award sentiment, and assist-driven offensive control. He belongs near the very top of the board.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Projected as Michigan State's starting point guard in Big Ten and rotation previews.
teamContext.returningStars: Fears is framed as the skeleton key for a top Big Ten backcourt.
teamContext.incomingStars: Michigan State has guard depth, but it supports spacing and secondary creation rather than threatening his role.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Jordan Scott, Kur Teng, and young guards compete around him; none displace his primary lead-guard role.
teamContext.starterEvidence: projected-lineup reporting and national award/odds context
teamContext.roleCap: No practical cap; role can be 100 because the usage and leadership evidence are both elite.

evidence.notes: Projects Fears in Michigan State's starting lineup and frames him as a potential preseason Big Ten Player of the Year.
Projects Fears as the starting point guard and cites a nation-leading assist profile.
Early Wooden odds context lists Fears as the favorite.

players.researchSummary: Sandfort is a proven high-major scorer and projected Nebraska starter. The role and offensive burden should be extremely high, but the NBA/upside score is kept below the true lottery/NPOY tier, which keeps him strong without forcing him into the absolute top group.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Nebraska projected-lineup coverage has Sandfort as a starting guard/wing and focal scorer.
teamContext.returningStars: He is one of Nebraska's clear lead offensive pieces with Braden Frager also involved.
teamContext.incomingStars: Nebraska added pieces, but the roster still points toward Sandfort as a primary scoring option.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Competition is more about complementary usage than minutes.
teamContext.starterEvidence: projected starting lineup and leading-scorer context
teamContext.roleCap: Near-primary scorer; cap just under Fears/Haugh because the national-player signal is a touch lighter.

evidence.notes: Projects Sandfort in Nebraska's starting group and cites elite scoring/shooting production.
Rothstein projects Sandfort in Nebraska's starting five.

players.researchSummary: Moore is a high-confidence returning mid-major star with efficient volume scoring. The role and burden are very high, but the NBA/upside score is moderated because this is more proven college dominance than draft-driven upside.

teamContext.projectedLineup: WKU returnee coverage frames Moore as the heart of the roster.
teamContext.returningStars: Moore is WKU's best all-around player and primary scoring option.
teamContext.incomingStars: Roster turnover increases his importance rather than crowding him.
teamContext.positionCompetition: No meaningful role threat if healthy.
teamContext.roleCap: High-90s role, but final BBPR remains anchored below major-conference NPOY tier.

evidence.notes: Lists Moore's All-CUSA/NABC honors and 18.7 PPG, 5.6 RPG efficiency profile.
Frames Moore as WKU's best all-around player entering 2026-27.

players.researchSummary: Falslev is a very strong returning college player with major career production, especially as a two-way guard. His final projection should respect the historical anchor, but the talent/upside grade is below the NPOY and NBA-forward tiers.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Utah State roster context points to Falslev as a returning starter and program anchor.
teamContext.returningStars: He owns major career accumulation in points, assists, steals, games, and starts.
teamContext.incomingStars: No newcomer meaningfully threatens his role.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Role is secure; offensive burden may be shared depending on roster growth.
teamContext.starterEvidence: career starts and school roster/career record context
teamContext.roleCap: High-90s role, with BBPR anchored by historical rating and moderated NBA signal.

evidence.notes: Lists Falslev's senior roster status and career records in points, assists, steals, games, and starts.

players.researchSummary: De Ridder gets a major returning-player boost because he was already Virginia's leading scorer/rebounder, is part of a stable returning core, and has sophomore development runway despite being an older international player.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Virginia coverage projects De Ridder as a returning starter and core piece.
teamContext.returningStars: He led Virginia in points and rebounds and made All-ACC First Team.
teamContext.incomingStars: Virginia added depth but the returning core remains central.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Frontcourt depth helps lineup quality more than it threatens his role.

evidence.notes: Virginia returns De Ridder as a core starter; he led the team in scoring and rebounding.
Lists All-ACC First Team honors and 15.6 PPG, 6.2 RPG production.

players.researchSummary: Mirkovic has one of the best year-two breakout cases in the country: productive freshman season, Final Four team context, Keaton Wagler's departure, and early Wooden odds. He should be safely above most freshmen and in the upper returning-star tier.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Illinois context points toward Mirkovic as a featured frontcourt starter.
teamContext.returningStars: He is a likely focal point after Keaton Wagler's departure.
teamContext.incomingStars: Illinois has talent around him, but that should boost efficiency more than lower role.
teamContext.starterEvidence: Wooden odds, returning production, and expected focal-point coverage
teamContext.roleCap: High-90s role because the national sentiment is unusually strong.

evidence.notes: Reports Mirkovic with the third-best early Wooden odds and expected bigger role.
Identifies Illinois' need for a new go-to scorer with Mirkovic among the leading options.

players.researchSummary: Ngongba is a high-upside returning Duke starter candidate. His role should grow, and NBA sentiment is real, but Duke has enough talent around him that offensive burden should sit below primary-option forwards.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Duke roster coverage frames Ngongba as the returning center with an expanded role.
teamContext.returningStars: One of Duke's most important returners and a possible 2027 lottery/first-round candidate.
teamContext.incomingStars: Duke's recruiting class adds talent but does not directly remove his center role.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Frontcourt depth exists, but Ngongba's size and experience give him the cleanest center path.
teamContext.starterEvidence: returning starting center and draft-return reporting
teamContext.roleCap: Mid-90s role; burden capped because Duke will have multiple creators.

evidence.notes: Reports Ngongba returning after borderline first-round draft consideration and expecting a larger role.
Frames Ngongba as a larger-role returner and 2027 draft candidate.

players.researchSummary: Chinyelu is a locked-in starter and national defensive anchor on the preseason No. 1 type roster. His role and confidence are elite, while offensive burden is intentionally lower because Florida has several frontcourt and guard stars.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Florida returns a loaded frontcourt with Chinyelu as a starting defensive centerpiece.
teamContext.returningStars: Chinyelu is the reigning national defensive player of the year.
teamContext.incomingStars: Florida's returners create shared usage but make the lineup stronger.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Condon and Haugh share frontcourt creation, limiting Chinyelu's offensive burden.
teamContext.starterEvidence: school return announcement and defensive player of the year profile
teamContext.roleCap: Role can be elite, but final score remains below high-usage offensive stars.

evidence.notes: Reports Chinyelu returning after averaging a double-double and starting every Florida game over two seasons.
Reports Chinyelu winning Naismith Defensive Player of the Year.
Ranks Florida No. 1 with frontcourt returnees including Chinyelu.

players.researchSummary: Huff's role rises because Gonzaga has lost guard stability and needs him as a senior frontcourt anchor. Injury and roster uncertainty prevent a bigger jump, but he should be a projected starter and one of Gonzaga's top options.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Post-departure Gonzaga projections still include Huff as a starting forward.
teamContext.returningStars: Huff is one of the few key returning pieces and an All-America candidate in local coverage.
teamContext.incomingStars: Massamba Diop and others share frontcourt usage.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Frontcourt role is secure, but guard uncertainty could affect efficiency.
teamContext.starterEvidence: projected starting lineup and Gonzaga roster-context reporting
teamContext.roleCap: Starter/high-option role, with confidence capped by team uncertainty.

evidence.notes: Frames Huff as a senior All-America candidate and key returning Gonzaga frontcourt piece.
Projects Huff in Gonzaga's updated starting lineup after Mario Saint-Supery's departure.

players.researchSummary: Folgueiras was just below the top-100 line despite a productive forward profile and Louisville frontcourt opportunity. The corrected anchor keeps him in the high-major starter band.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Rothstein projects Folgueiras in Louisville's starting five.
teamContext.returningStars: Louisville has multiple perimeter and frontcourt options.
teamContext.incomingStars: Bidunga, Shelstad, Wooley, and Knox all create usage competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Likely starter, but not a guaranteed primary offensive option.
teamContext.starterEvidence: Folgueiras was just below the top-100 line despite a productive forward profile and Louisville frontcourt opportunity. The corrected anchor keeps him in the high-major starter band.
teamContext.roleCap: Corrected so role, anchor, and current trajectory are comparable across the full rated pool.
teamContext.scoredAnchor: Synthetic historical anchor: 74. Updated in the July 23 top-100 calibration pass.

evidence.notes: Official roster context verifies Folgueiras with Louisville.

players.researchSummary: Williams has elite long-term talent and draft upside, but Duke's veteran/returning roster points to a complementary freshman role rather than immediate top-board dominance. This is exactly the type of freshman who should be mixed into the board, not stacked above proven stars.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Duke coverage says Williams has a chance to start, but expected-minutes reporting points closer to a 17-20 MPG role.
teamContext.returningStars: Duke returns Foster, Boozer, Ngongba, Sarr, and other key rotation pieces.
teamContext.incomingStars: Duke's class is deep, but Williams is the top incoming freshman.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Wing/frontcourt competition keeps role below the elite-starting freshman tier.
teamContext.starterEvidence: possible starter, but minutes projection is not top-option level
teamContext.roleCap: Low-80s role until projected starting evidence strengthens.

evidence.notes: Lists Williams as Duke's No. 4 overall recruit and possible Day 1 starter.
Ranks Williams second among Duke freshmen for expected minutes at roughly 17-20 MPG.

players.researchSummary: Gaskins is a five-star forward with stronger immediate-role evidence than many freshmen because projected lineups place him in Miami's starting group. The newcomer adjustment still keeps him below established stars, but he should not be buried.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Rothstein projects Gaskins in Miami's starting five.
teamContext.returningStars: Miami has several new pieces and less settled hierarchy.
teamContext.incomingStars: Acaden Lewis and other newcomers create shared usage.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Likely starting path, but freshman volatility and team transition lower confidence.
teamContext.starterEvidence: projected starting lineup plus five-star signing context
teamContext.roleCap: Mid-80s role due clean lineup evidence but unclear usage hierarchy.

evidence.notes: Miami announced Gaskins as a five-star signee with top-25 recruiting rankings and strong EYBL/Peach Jam production.
Rothstein projects Gaskins in Miami's starting lineup.

players.researchSummary: Suigo has size and projected-starting lineup evidence, but the overall talent/upside and usage profile should be treated as developmental. He belongs in the mixed newcomer/rotation pocket, not above proven producers.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Rothstein projects Suigo as Villanova's starting center.
teamContext.returningStars: Villanova has veteran forwards and guards who can carry more offense.
teamContext.incomingStars: Kwame Evans and Devin Royal create frontcourt usage competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Center role is possible, but offensive burden should stay modest.
teamContext.starterEvidence: projected lineup and official roster size/profile
teamContext.roleCap: Low-80s role because the starting path is plausible but usage is uncertain.

evidence.notes: Lists Suigo as a 7-foot-3 freshman center from Italy/BC Mega Basket.
Rothstein projects Suigo in Villanova's starting lineup.

players.researchSummary: Diane is an elite freshman center prospect, but Houston's system and frontcourt standards usually make freshman offensive burden difficult to project aggressively. His talent is high; role certainty is not high enough yet for a top-board freshman score.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Recruiting evidence is strong, but direct Houston starting-lineup evidence is not yet strong enough.
teamContext.returningStars: Houston's physical veteran system usually demands defensive trust before huge freshman minutes.
teamContext.incomingStars: Diane is the headline frontcourt newcomer.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Center minutes depend on readiness, fouls, and defensive fit.
teamContext.starterEvidence: elite recruit, not yet clean projected starter
teamContext.roleCap: Role capped at 80 pending lineup evidence.

evidence.notes: Lists Diane as a Houston signee, top center, and national top-20 prospect.
Reports Diane committing to Houston as a top-ranked big in the 2026 class.

players.researchSummary: Keita has lottery-caliber international upside and fills a real UNC frontcourt need, but UNC has multiple frontcourt newcomers and Samodurov/Stevenson competition. Treat him as a high-upside mixed-in newcomer until the starting center picture is clearer.

teamContext.projectedLineup: UNC frontcourt has a need, but available reporting still frames the center minutes as competitive.
teamContext.returningStars: Jarin Stevenson and other returners/newcomers affect frontcourt hierarchy.
teamContext.incomingStars: Samodurov, Keita, and other newcomers create role competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Center minutes are available but not guaranteed to one player.
teamContext.starterEvidence: major international commitment and roster need, but not clean starter evidence
teamContext.roleCap: Low-80s role due high upside but unresolved frontcourt distribution.

evidence.notes: Reports Keita's UNC commitment and cites projected 2027 lottery-pick type upside.
Lists Keita as a five-star, top-20 national/No. 3 center prospect committed to UNC.
